requirements_for_qualification.summary: Must install qualifying energy-efficient improvements to principal residence to claim Inflation Reduction Act enhanced credits: 30% for solar/geothermal and up to $3,200 annually for heat pumps, insulation, and efficient HVAC under expanded provisions
criteria: Energy Efficient Home Improvement Credit (25C): Up to $3,200 annually for qualifying improvements
Eligible improvements: heat pumps, heat pump water heaters, biomass stoves, insulation, windows, doors, electrical panel upgrades
Annual limits 25C: $1,200 for most items; $2,000 for heat pumps; combined $3,200 maximum per year
Residential Clean Energy Credit (25D): 30% of cost for solar panels, solar water heaters, geothermal heat pumps, small wind, fuel cells
No annual or lifetime limit on 25D credit; can carry forward unused credit if exceeds tax liability
Installation must be at taxpayer's principal residence in the United States
Must meet specific energy efficiency requirements and obtain Manufacturer Certification Statement
Credits available through 2032 for solar (30% through 2032); 25C credit permanent but subject to annual limits

energy_credit_rules.25c_annual_limit: $1,200 general; $2,000 heat pumps; $3,200 combined maximum per year
energy_credit_rules.25d_no_limit: 30% of solar, geothermal, small wind, fuel cells with no annual cap
energy_credit_rules.ira_enhancement: IRA increased 25C limits and extended 25D credit at 30% through 2032
energy_credit_rules.principal_residence: Must be installed at main home; vacation homes don't qualify for 25C (may qualify for 25D)
energy_credit_rules.carryforward_available: Unused 25D credit carries forward; 25C credit cannot be carried forward
energy_credit_rules.installation_year: Credit claimed in year property placed in service, not when paid

restrictions: 25C credit: annual limits apply per taxpayer, not per property or per improvement type
Cannot exceed tax liability for year (non-refundable credit); 25D carryforward available, 25C does not carry forward
Must obtain and retain Manufacturer Certification Statement proving efficiency requirements met
Rental property and business use don't qualify for residential credits; different credits may apply
Solar panels: must be installed on or in connection with dwelling you use as residence
Installation labor included in 25D credit; not included in 25C credit (equipment only)
Cannot claim credit for improvements financed by subsidized energy financing or utility rebates
State and local incentives may be available in addition to federal credits; coordinate for maximum benefit
